title: GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite
text:
Note:

Hi, I am Abusyeed. I designed this landing page as a solo UX designer during a 48-hour hackathon organized by GUVI. I completed the end-to-end design, modeling, and presentation within this timeline.

## 01 Introduction

GreenBite is a fictional service aiming to replace unhealthy eating habits with freshly packed, nutritious organic food delivered directly to users. The goal of this project was to design a highly engaging, conversion-optimized landing page, establishing a strong brand identity and introducing custom product packaging within a 48-hour hackathon.

### Challenge

The challenge was to design a clean, minimal landing page for GreenBite, including custom logo branding, desktop/tablet/mobile layouts, and product details. The core task was to communicate the brand's health values clearly to busy professionals while driving actions via highly visible CTAs.

### Goal

Develop a responsive, visually appealing landing page that builds immediate user trust, clearly presents organic food benefits, and guides user exploration with a clean UI.

## 02 User Research

Due to the 48-hour hackathon timeline, I conducted rapid user persona mapping to understand our core target segments.

### Personas

:::personas
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 2.png)
The Health-Conscious Professional
- **Mindset:** Wants clean, organic meals but lacks the time to cook due to long working hours.
- **Pain Points:** Hectic lifestyle, high reliance on fast food, difficulty finding reliable organic meal subscriptions.
- **Needs:** Quick ordering, transparent nutritional tables, and trust-building social proof.
:::

### Corporate Trapped

These users lead demanding professional lives and have very little time to prepare healthy food. GreenBite serves as a convenient solution to deliver organic, nutritious options daily, tailored to their dietary preferences.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 3.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 4.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 5.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 6.png)

## 03 Ideation & Design

After mapping the persona's frustrations, I brainstormed layouts to address their primary needs. I selected shades of green to match the organic and sustainable theme, pairing it with a clean, minimal header menu.

### Brand Identity & Custom Modeling

- **Wordmark Logo:** I designed a simplified logo by adjusting font weights and spacing to visually emphasize the words "Green" and "Bite."
- **Custom Takeout Box:** To make the packaging feel real and premium, I modeled a custom takeout box in Adobe Dimension, wrapping it with nutritional details, product names, and pricing labels.

:::grid
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Logo_Explanation.png)
Logo and Brand Structure
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Food_Packet.png)
Custom modeled Takeout Box
:::

### Visual Layout Rationale

- **Hero Header:** Created a prominent hero banner containing the primary value proposition to quickly convey the service to first-time visitors. The primary CTA button is placed dead-center to encourage instant conversion.
- **Social Proof:** Integrated customer reviews alongside product renderings to build immediate trust and lower user hesitation.
- **Product Storefront:** Added a list of popular items highlighting tags like "Top Seller" and "Value Pack" to guide decision making.
- **Responsive Layout:** Designed components with defined hover/active states, custom header underlines for easy navigation, and a collapsable mobile hamburger menu.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Untitled 7.png)

:::grid
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Colors.png)
Color Palette
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Typography.png)
Typography Guidelines
:::

### Layout & Wireframes

:::grid
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Tab_Wireframe.png)
Tablet Wireframe
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Mobile_Wireframe.png)
Mobile Wireframe
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Lo-Fi_Wireframe.png)
Low Fidelity Layout
:::

### Final Screens & Design Rationale

:::showcase
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_17.png)
01 Hero Header Section
- **Central Logo & Navigation:** Centered logo to instantly establish branding, paired with a minimal menu to keep the initial cognitive load extremely low.
- **Bold Value Proposition:** Headline clearly outlines the organic benefit, placed immediately to capture the user's attention.
- **High-Contrast CTA:** The prominent CTA button sits dead center, prompting immediate conversion.
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_14.png)
02 Value Props & Trust
- **Interactive Review Card:** A highlighted customer review card is placed here to build immediate trust and credibility.
- **Visual Food Model:** An image of a fresh takeout box shows the tangible product, making the service feel real and high quality.
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_15.png)
03 Organic Benefits
- **Nutrition Information Grid:** A clean layout showcasing the ingredients and vitamins, proving the organic claim.
- **Clear Copywriting:** Emphasizes empowering users with nutritious alternatives to standard fast food.
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Group_16.png)
04 Product Storefront
- **Top Seller Badges:** Urgency cues like "Top Seller" and "Value Pack" are used on specific cards to guide purchase decisions.
- **Clean Card Hierarchy:** Organized product details, showing price and cart CTAs at the bottom of each card.
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Footer.png)
05 Footer Section
- **Empathetic Brand Mission:** A summary of the brand's goal to eradicate fast food culture, closing the user journey with a positive message.
- **Structured Links:** Organized footer columns for clean, functional bottom-page navigation.
:::

## 04 Testing

I evaluated the layout using an AI attention heatmap plugin to analyze how visual attention is distributed across the page, yielding a high overall landing page score of 66.

:::heatmap
Headline - 34.5%
Image - 25.6%
Primary CTA - 4.8%
Logo - 2.8%
Secondary CTA - 1.4%
:::

The attention insights confirm that the headline and main graphics successfully draw the initial eye share, validating the visual hierarchy.

:::grid
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/heatmap.png)
Attention Heatmap
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/GUVI - Dan Jr Hackathon - GreenBite/Rectangle.png)
Attention Area Maps
:::

## 05 Recollect

Designing this page under a 48-hour constraint taught me how to rapidly map user preferences, build models, and test design ideas. Re-utilizing Adobe Dimension allowed me to quickly design custom packaging mockups, producing a polished, comprehensive interface presentation.

title: Kynhood - UX & AI
text:
## 01 Introduction

In this UX document, I have enhanced the zone and area selection feature of a social media application where users couldn't select their area or view the multiple zones it covers. I applied my solution in the context of the Kynhood app, while also keeping it relevant to a generic social media platform. The document covers multiple solutions, spanning both UI design and development-based approaches.

:::meta
Role | Product Designer
Members | Solo
Duration | 48 Hours
Tools | Figma, Notion, Jupyter Notebook
Coffee | 5 Cups
:::

https://www.figma.com/design/j2YLGjFpBMny0kUMs7jAyf/KYN?node-id=0-1&t=Xdz64VXLAnzX5TbL-1

## 02 Problem Statement

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled.png)

Source - Public Information Portal, Chennai

The key insight from reading the brief was that users couldn't see which specific areas each zone covers, leading to confusion during onboarding. The problem also called for designing with accessibility in mind. The problem statement had a few specific requirements to address:

- **Zone and Area Clarity** — Design a solution that clearly displays all available zones and the respective areas they cover, ensuring users can easily understand their choices.

- **User-friendly Interaction** — The method for selecting a zone should be straightforward, minimising steps and complexity for the user.

- **Scalability** — The design should accommodate future expansion, allowing for the addition of more zones and areas as the service grows into new cities and states.

- **Intuitive Design** — The screen should intuitively convey its purpose and how to use it, ensuring users understand they are selecting a zone to receive content relevant to that area.

## 03 Initial Finding

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Image_16.png)

To solve this, I first needed to understand how the city divides its geography into zones, divisions, and wards. I reviewed several websites that map city divisions and zones, and after thorough research, arrived at a workable approach. The government's geographic structure assigns a list of wards to each zone. Previously, wards could span multiple zones under parliamentary or assembly constituencies, but the newly proposed zones are distinct from one another.

Every 2,000 to 3,000 households form a ward, and most people identify primarily with their local area name rather than their ward or zone — and that is where the problem lies. Since users only know the area they live in, they struggle to identify which zone it belongs to.

## 04 Ideation

I quickly sketched how zones connect to areas, which resulted in a tree structure with multiple areas linked to each zone. Since people know the local area they live in, taking that as input and mapping it to a zone would help users easily identify their correct zone.

This problem leaned more toward a development solution than a pure design one. As a product designer, I wanted to understand how the backend systems could align with and support a seamless user experience.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 1.png)

Source - Wikipedia

I referenced Chennai's wards and zones from Wikipedia. As you can see in the image above, both the old and new divisions are visible, which will help surface relevant neighbourhood content to users.

For instance,

If you look closely at the ward numbers, they are not continuous or sequential. Wards missing from one zone's range fall under the adjacent zone. For example, wards 15–17 are absent from Zone 1 and instead belong to Zone 2. By mapping these wards to their respective zones in a database, users would be able to select their zone with ease. For users who live near a zone border, it is better to surface the nearest-neighbour zones and let them choose which zones they want content from.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 2.png)

Source - Chennai Greater Corporation

I cross-referenced wards and zones from Chennai Corporation and found that area names are uniquely tied to ward numbers. When a locality exceeds 3,000 households, it gets a new ward number. Each zone remains distinct regardless of ward boundaries.

| Ward No | Zone No | Area | Zone | Nearest Zone |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | 1 | KATHIVAKKAM | THIRUVOTRIYUR | THIRUVOTRIYUR |
| 15 | 2 | EDYANCHAVADI | MANALI | THIRUVOTRIYUR, MANALI |
| 32 | 2 | KAVANKARA | MADHAVARAM | MANALI, MADHAVARAM |

## Design

I built the designs using a design system I created during my previous internship. I designed for all user types, and to reduce cognitive load, I used familiar input fields to capture user input. The flow starts with the user's known area as input, then maps it to the corresponding zone.

### User Flow

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 3.png)

User flow

This is the most direct path to the goal — location selection has only two components, and keeping it to these elements helps users reach their goal quickly.

The entry point is the location page, where users type their area name — the most familiar piece of information they know. After typing, a dropdown presents matching area options grouped under their common zone.

### Wireframe

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Image_17.png)

Once the ideas came together, I began sketching rough wireframes covering most test cases, deciding which concepts to keep and which to drop. With the design system already in place, assembling the components took only about 5 minutes.

https://www.figma.com/design/3XEIfBgCzEuCkfnDGq4Li1/Design-System?node-id=132-8171&t=UcpCXACaA1QbXkps-1 - 786920

### High Fidelity

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 4.png)

1: An input field with a label and helper text to keep the user informed, plus a leading icon prompting them to type and search for their area.

2: Below the area input is the zone input. People naturally fill input fields top to bottom, so this linear order aligns with existing mental models.

3: As the user types, a dropdown offers matching area options — tolerant of minor typos. A subheading above each result shows the common zone name, since multiple areas can share the same name.

4: Helper text keeps the user informed of their next step — selecting from the available choices.

A tooltip summarising the flow in a minimal way would further guide users.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 5.png)

6: Once the user types their area, nearby zones appear and they can choose from them. This gives users the freedom to select their preferred zone. Most users will receive content from the single zone they live in, but those near zone borders may want content from both sides.

7: Users select their zone using a checkbox. I chose this control because of its familiar mental model — checkboxes are universally understood across age groups for multi-select scenarios. Unlike a toggle, a checkbox is designed for a selection that can be revisited and changed later in the process.

8: The CTA button activates only once both the area and zone are filled in.

9: Users can review their selections in both the input field and a tag group, making it easy to remove any entry they do not need.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 6.png)

10: This shows the case where only one zone appears in the dropdown.

## 06 Suggestions

### AI

This approach works well for structured cities with defined zones. However, if zone boundaries change dynamically, the mappings would need to be updated accordingly. To avoid the overhead of manual updates, ML algorithms can handle this automatically.

I proposed using KNN — a common algorithm for finding the nearest points between clusters of data. We can implement a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifier to find the nearest zones based on a given ward number. It works by calculating the distance between a ward's coordinates and other wards in the dataset. The algorithm then identifies the K nearest data points and assigns the majority class among them to the input. The KNN classifier determines the nearest zones to a given ward by considering its neighbouring wards and their corresponding zones.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/k-nearest-neighbor-algorithm-for-machine-learning11.png)

Source - Javatpoint

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 7.png)

Source - Google

Code

```python
import numpy as np
from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier

zones = {
    "THIRUVOTRIYUR": range(1, 14),
    "MANALI": range(15, 21),
    "MADHAVARAM": range(22, 33),
    "TONDIARPET": range(34, 48),
    "ROYAPURAM": range(49, 63),
    "THIRUVIKA NAGAR": range(64, 78),
    "AMBATTUR": range(79, 93),
    "ANNA NAGAR": range(94, 108),
    "TEYNAMPET": range(109, 126),
    "KODAMBAKKAM": range(127, 142),
    "VALASARAVAKKAM": range(143, 155),
    "ALANDUR": range(156, 167),
    "ADYAR": range(170, 182),
    "PERUNGUDI": list(range(168, 169)) + list(range(183, 191)),
    "SOZHANGANALLUR": range(192, 200)
}

ward_numbers = []
zone_names = []

for zone, wards in zones.items():
    for ward in wards:
        ward_numbers.append(ward)
        zone_names.append(zone)

X = np.array(ward_numbers).reshape(-1, 1)
y = np.array(zone_names)

knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=3)
knn.fit(X, y)

def classify_ward(ward_number):
    ward_number = np.array(ward_number).reshape(-1, 1)
    prediction = knn.predict(ward_number)
    neighbors = knn.kneighbors(ward_number, return_distance=False)
    nearest_zones = list(set([y[i] for i in neighbors[0]]))
    return prediction[0], nearest_zones

ward_number = int(input("Enter ward number: "))
zone, nearest_zones = classify_ward(ward_number)
print(f"Ward Number: {ward_number}")
print(f"Zone: {zone}")
print(f"Nearest Zones: {nearest_zones}")
```

```output
Enter ward number: 14  # User types their area e.g. RajaKadai
Ward Number: 14
Zone: THIRUVOTRIYUR
Nearest Zones: ['MANALI', 'THIRUVOTRIYUR']  # User gets choice to select
```

As the output shows, when a user types their known area, the system quickly determines the correct zone and the nearest neighbouring zones. It does require developer effort to maintain and extend mappings across different cities, but as the method demonstrates, zone identification is both fast and accurate.

### Maps

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Kynhood - UX & AI/Untitled 8.png)

Adding a map to the interface would greatly help users who have recently moved to a city. As the user types their area, the map could display a pin and highlight the ward boundary, making it easy to visually confirm their location and select the correct zone.

## 07 Conclusion

This project gave me the opportunity to explore geographic specifications I was previously unfamiliar with — wards, divisions, and zones, and how they are partitioned. It was a uniquely different challenge compared to my previous internship work.

On the topic of scaling, zone boundaries can shift over time, and manually updating datasets each time is not sustainable. Leveraging ML algorithms for dynamic zone mapping would be far more efficient and scalable.

title: PhonePe 2.0 - BTS
text:
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/PhonePe 2 0 - BTS/Group_481509.png)

# PhonePe 2.0 - BTS

## Verdict [Time Saver]

PhonePe's sudden UI revamp faced backlash as users, especially merchants, struggled with the abrupt change. Disrupting muscle memory risks frustration, making gradual updates preferable.

The old design, though cluttered, was familiar, while the new bento grid layout feels modern and structured. Multi-language support has improved, but dark mode needs accessibility refinements.

UPI apps share similar layouts due to NPCI guidelines and Jakob's Law, ensuring familiarity and fair competition. Users rarely switch apps due to habit and trust. PhonePe's redesign aligns with competitors but must balance innovation with user comfort.

## Users Don't like Change

When the PhonePe interface was changed overnight, the internet was flooded with comments. As a result, fewer people loved it, and most people didn't like it at all. As far as I'm aware, PhonePe has a sizeable market share, and the majority of its users are business owners, merchants, and store owners because it provides business and merchant apps.

Users dislike abrupt changes, which will impact their muscle memory, according to design principles. In the worst case scenario, they will quit using the app.

They started commenting on people's rant on X, They replied saying they wanted to improve:

- Navigation

- Modern Look

- Accessibility

- Usability

Changes should be made gradually or significantly, in my opinion, as users may consider switching to other platforms or being convinced by their friends if they are made abruptly. A few user-submitted ideas based on the issue:

- Having a back button to Classic UI — This might enable to bring back the old UI completely

- Parent Mode — This helps people to drive easy payment or the features they only need because they have very less technical knowledge

The techy, young and gen-z people might get easy in a week to use and only if they have any dependency like insurance and auto-pay else they will be the first to change the platform and thats separate use case but the old generation people who use this will surely get hit and get so much confused even if they have changed the design overnight.

Every design will end up in the hands of consumers, and even if it has defects, if it solves a problem, they will continue to use it, which is how muscle memory is developed. Now suddenly you modify the interface and keep stuff somewhere, it comes out as a problem.

## 1.0 - Before

For the majority of Indians, PhonePe is the preferred UPI app due to its well-organised list of requirements for everyday tasks. They had sections of sponsored apps, a large carousel for running advertising, and everything that brought them business was in the navigation bar.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/PhonePe 2 0 - BTS/Group_481511.png)

Year back they changed their position of the QR from header to navigation bar and it helped people to click with ease and pay. This one interaction went viral and people applauded for their efforts making easy for users.

It had a categorised rectangle boxes of:

- Recharge & Pay bills

- Loan

- Insurance

These do have sub categories, which again they listed upfront with the icons. This was actually easy for people who can use what exactly they want to avail the service but yet found upfront.

The older design has lot of google ads, sponsored content so It made the app more dense and cluttered. Most users say this is very clear and clutter free because they adopted to use the interface and when someone who use it for the first time will find uneasy.

## After 2.0

This new revamp gives lot of trust and clarity in my opinion. First which I notice is the elegance which I get than the older design layout, and it is clean and neat.

In their old design, We could see the lot of subcategories upfront, But here in the revamp the categories and clubbed into minimal cards — which is called bento grids. Bento box is a Japanese tradition of packing food into box.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/PhonePe 2 0 - BTS/Group_481512.png)

Depending on their priorities and the size that brings them the most revenue, they have maintained a variety of box sizes. They had distinct icons for each of the subcategories in the previous design, but now that they are claiming to be redesigning it for accessibility, they have included the features in the box itself.

This revamp also helps to have multiple language support with ease. For example we have gpay on the right and phonePe on left. If I change language it wraps text but the phonePe design is good for multiple languages and accessible for everyone. The information architecture of the cards are well structured, based on the features where they get good business. Now in the each box they have a cool illustration which is subtle and elegant to explain what the card contains.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/PhonePe 2 0 - BTS/Group_481513.png)

## PhonePe and DS

PhonePe has design system and they do have multiple apps like Pincode and Merchant apps but there is lookout in those apps. Every app don't follow same design system because they don't follow same foundation. Every app has a connection to generic design system but the components they use in every app is different and purpose as well.

They recently rolled out dark mode to their new UI, It has rendered good but few places they can work on accessibility wise because UPI apps are for everybody.

They only way to have dark and light mode is using design system, and they have been using this very recently and I heard that they follow design system and influence more on design first.

## Design for UPI Apps

If you can see all the UPI apps have very similar UI, One of the reasons from a pure play design stand point will be Jakob's Law which states that the people who use a niche category app which serves almost same service may expect same service anyway.

There is a catch again, These apps are structured and made with almost similar UI as a part of NPCI guidelines. NPCI's Volume cap guideline — OC97, Where they have constraint to limit the transactions to 30% around all apps. NPCI has analyzed the risks in the UPI ecosystem. To address the risks and protect the UPI ecosystem they have rolled out these guidelines and you can wonder why we have so many apps for UPI with almost everything same layout.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/PhonePe 2 0 - BTS/Group_481510.png)

Lot of apps might have same features and functionality just because of this NPCI guidelines are meant to follow, they do roll out brand guidelines for their logo, so it doesn't confuse people. In verdict designing for UPI apps also need to have same layout over all other apps and they follow due to this. Leave design, they should also features like UPI lite, Autopay and other features which they have.

Surprisingly users who use UPI apps don't change the app they use, because they will find difficult using unfamiliar layouts and features. As we use it daily and we stick to a muscle memory, trust and ease. Users find their current UPI is sufficient enough to use and there is no alternative is significantly better. So PhonePe has tried to make a similar one with their competitors but yet simple layout and this is how the design is evolved.

title: Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed
text:
### Introduction

I have made a detailed write up on enhancement of the advanced search filters and the boolean search. As it was the problem statement and the first step I started going through all the features of the Recruit CRM.

### The Initial Findings

I am currently studying ML and AI, I made a fake CSV sheet through python and Faker Libraries. I imported all those in the Recruit CRM to know more on how they do so. In specific they gave me a free trail so I can only add fake profiles to 50 alone with a sample profile called “William”.

The most fascinating part of Recruit CRM is its office blue tone on the whole dashboard which gives me to stay on the page. So far I have seen these recruiting excel sheets and other websites it doesn’t feel the same.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/A4_-_1.png)

Dashboard comparison with Heatmap based on CTA’s.

When I import those candidate excel sheets in the dashboard, I got a side page to map all the column with Recruiter CRM’s columns, So it made more easy to import only the given and others can be ignored from the excel. Every result which I get is very late although I have a good spec PC and a good internet.

### Understanding Current Advanced Search:

After all profiles were imported, I explored the filters to surface any usability errors. Since the task was to enhance advanced search filters and Boolean search, I conducted a competitive analysis to understand how similar products handle this.

### Competitive Analysis

:::competitors
Zoho Recruit | https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=zohorecruit.com&sz=64
Bullhorn | https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=bullhorn.com&sz=64
Pipedrive | https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=pipedrive.com&sz=64
Manatal | https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=manatal.com&sz=64
RecruiterFlow | https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?domain=recruiterflow.com&sz=64
:::

These are the primary alternatives to Recruit CRM, each with multiple features. Recruit CRM stands out through its distinctive blue-and-green UI. I tested Zoho Recruit using its free version. Manatal is particularly comparable to Recruit CRM, as it also supports an advanced Boolean search with a range-based skill filter.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/Flow_chart.png)

Manatal Skill Level Search

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/Flow_chart2.png)

Recruit CRM Filter search

### Identifying Usability Concerns

Usability encompasses factors such as learnability and efficiency. I analyzed these to optimize the design. To better understand the problem, I first examined why Boolean operators are used in search. The operators AND, OR, and NOT are heavily used in Recruit CRM's advanced search. A key constraint is that OR and NOT must be written in uppercase block letters to function correctly.

![Image](https://downloads.intercomcdn.com/i/o/122973423/def660c263b325ff803c0943/ezgif.com-gif-maker.png)

Image Source: Recruit CRM

In the above image, Recruit CRM claims to support a simplified Boolean syntax by removing brackets and quotes. However, this only works when the operators are written in uppercase. When searching for candidates with diverse technical backgrounds, using OR and NOT in the correct case is essential.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/Screenshot_2020-04-02_at_11.55_1.png)

Image Source: Recruit CRM

I ran this test using a free trial with 50 imported candidate profiles.

| Query | Contains Search | Result |
| Canada OR Turkey | No | Result found |
| canada or turkey | Yes | No result found |
| canada OR turkey | No | Result found |

Case-sensitive Boolean expressions create significant confusion for users. I personally experienced this friction on my first attempt. When composing a long Boolean string across multiple columns, even a single lowercase operator silently returns zero results.

### Ideation - Optimizing search

After uploading all candidate profiles, the system should scan each profile and auto-populate filter fields with relevant details. For example, if a company only operates in China, all city and state fields should reflect that automatically. I proposed combining Boolean search with filter-based search into a unified interface, making it easier to locate candidates by selecting options from dropdowns rather than composing raw SQL-style Boolean strings.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/A4_-_2.png)

Comparing Wireframe and Product

### Wireframe

I created a wireframe prototype of the redesigned advanced search module. As discussed, the case-sensitivity issue significantly impacts efficiency and increases the chance of error. Since the dashboard already has a dedicated Advanced Search placement, the redesign focused on that area.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/notionimg2.png)

Old Layout - Advanced Search

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/notionimg.png)

Wireframe - Advanced Search

So far I have transformed few CTA’s and added new one’s for better efficiency in search employees, In addition the new one’s are dropdown menus. These (2-5) are termed to be mandatory fields where the recruiters assign them task, if any other than 4 listed dropdown to be searched then Boolean can help them to identify the distinct candidate.

The Boolean search is entirely for distinct skills for example -  “Designer” Not “Web Designer”, “J2EE” OR “SQL”. As the old design has the include search results which makes to see more results based on the search, So it is also indeed in this wireframe too.

:::grid
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/A4_-_3.png)
Clear Filters - Original
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/A4_-_4.png)
Reset Filters - Wireframe
:::

- **Reset Filters** — Relocated from the bottom action bar to sit adjacent to the Boolean search input, allowing recruiters to quickly clear filters when something goes wrong.

- Location  - This button is defined to specify exact city or office spaces the employees is working. It also has remote or working from home dropdown menu’s which makes user to tap and select their desired option.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/Frame_1.png)

Hide Fidelity - Advanced Search

- **Gender** — A dropdown populated from the uploaded CSV, listing the gender values of all imported employees.

- **Country** — Lists countries where offices are located, reflecting where employees work based on client arrangements. A radial location search could further enhance this in future iterations.

- **Language** — Lists all candidate languages as a dropdown, allowing recruiters to filter by desired language with a single tap.

- **Save and Search** — Designed without a redundant secondary button to improve accessibility and reduce decision fatigue, making the primary intent clear.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/confusion.png)

Multiple Colored Action button

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/confumeme.png)

Confusion Meme

As the Hick’s Law states, The time it takes to make a decision increases with the number and complexity Of choices.

- **Search** — The primary action button. It uses the brand's accent color to stand out visually, signaling to users that this will deliver the desired result.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 1 - Abusyeed/A4_-_5.png)

Hide Fidelity - Advanced Search

At last the required columns which has the second priority can be added in the filter search, So that it will make easy to some uncommon test cases. So the task was on the usability enhancement then the aspects such as Learnability, Efficiency, Error prevention, Satisfaction and Memorability can be defined through only by multiple iterations on the design phase by collecting feedback from users. As a fresher, I know the way test design but don’t have potential to do it. These ideation will surely increase in learnability and efficiency also in prevention of errors.

## Conclusion:

The proposed enhancements to Recruit CRM's advanced search feature have been outlined and the core usability concerns addressed. These refinements aim to improve recruiter efficiency and reduce errors caused by case-sensitive Boolean expressions.

title: Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed
text:
### Introduction

In this document, I have enhanced the header and adequate icons on all pages of Recruit CRM, which was very clumsy and confusing. I have made this very simple in order to get better accessibility. Problem statement is concise, where as a user I couldn’t find things which I want the more as to do tasks and there are lot of second priority elements occupying space at the first pace.

### Problem Statement

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/Untitled.png)

Maze - Result

To make this more accurate I picked my three known mates to go through my prototype, I used Maze to observe their behavior by some prompts and the results are in the above image. This result has no relation to friendliness bias or any other type, It has been derived only as a generic moderator.

### Initial Findings

As they are stick to website habits that help pages always be with profile dropdown menu, they tried to look there for help, but those are available next to advanced search. It has very low visibility rate, So it can be missed most of the time

It clearly shows that it is difficult to a new person to get at least help from the page exclusively, but there is a global help page and it needs to type down problems. Why would someone try to get sort out their problems globally than if it can be in their page itself.

I have seen many dashboard UI designs in the internet and they have good accent color to showcase aesthetics, I guess Recruit CRM has the same but the icons and the placement of the buttons are little difficult to the new users.

:::showcase
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/new2.png)
Help Icon Usability Issue
There is a misplaced icon in the help videos section. While it contains videos for that specific page, it doesn't resemble any sort of help icon. I updated this in the wireframe for better recognition.
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/new3.png)
Lock Icon Upgrade Warning
This button displays a dialog to upgrade to continue. However, the lock icon creates hesitation for new users to interact with it. It should instead redirect cleanly to the plans page.
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/new1.png)
Redundant Email Icon
Having an email composition option in this location is uncommon since there is a dedicated mailbox page. It has minimal impact on the workflow, as there are better ways to email an employee.
:::

### Ideation - Help Videos

The main concern is the users of Recruit CRM couldn’t access the help pages properly and it can be fixed by placing in a highly visible area, So it can be easily accessible. Every other competitor has distinct flow of work and if any new user switches to new product they will expect few mandatory things to be in certain places such as help pages.

Certainly I have designed a enhanced version of header of Recruit CRM, which has more importance on the usability part to make accessibility to all. Initially, I removed “What’s new”, “Add”, “Compose email” and made something different to meet easy learnability.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/taskb11.png)

Recruit CRM - Wireframe

I quickly change pages from the side bar, the header seems to be static and all other elements below the header loads according to page. So I only can do minimal changes and I did the placing different locations.

### Wireframe

The new designed wireframe is available at this (Link)

- Help Videos - This button is known to help button with this icon “❔”, It primarily solves users constraints and queries through videos. After action is declared same with side bar videos for that specific page.

:::grid
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/45.png)
Help Videos - Icon
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/46.png)
Help - Icon
:::

- Column Editor - This button in current design is next to advanced search and it is hidden, It can’t be accessible to new users because it is mixed with lot of buttons. The new placed location gives space to users and actual idea how this button functions.

- Profile - Here, a new item is added “What’s new” which gives updates on recruit CRM when users hit their profile page. Although flash boxes are shown in every update with its version.

- Notification - It is a highly indeed element and it only give all notification about literally everything.

- What’s New - In previous design, this element is placed in the static header as we discussed earlier it does shows updates on the users profile.

At last those elements in header are efficiently transformed, I guess these are the changes to make it clean and visually appealing. The main constraint I personally found was the help videos but I got links from my assessment link. The new users may get diverted or frustrated without knowing how go next, that’s why help videos should be shown more importance.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/taskb22.png)

What’s New - Wireframe

## Suggestions:

### Icons

The icons which are being used in the side bar are very diverse, such that some are know to be generic icons or some are from other icon family. In addition the icons should be equal, like to be filled icons over all pages but few area it doesn’t has filled icon. These doesn’t come in usability enhancements, but also needs to be changed in perspective of user interface.

:::grid
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/A4_-_6.png)
Billing Icon - Outline
---
![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/task_b1.png)
Calendar Icon - Outline
:::

### Grid

The changing grid to Kanban view in jobs and contacts page, but the placement of those grid options shouldn’t be in center? I have seen these type of changing grid view in e-commerce website to make shopping faster. This comparison is fully by the design trends which I seen in as a designer.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/kanban.png)

Grid Options - Recruit CRM

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Recruit CRM - UX Enhancement 2 - Abusyeed/ajio1.png)

Grid Option - AJIO

### Conclusion

Thanks for reading till the end! so far I have gone through recruit CRM, I have made my suggestions to improvise the overall usability by making a satisfaction to all type of users which is easy and pleasant.

title: Stimuler - UX Enhancement
text:
### Note

Hi I am Abu and I hope the reader is doing well. I am writing this document for the product design intern role at Stimuler and I have applied my minds in enhancing the profile tab and word of the day feature. This document focuses on the enhancement of features and specific section also it has solution which reach the direct metrics as well.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_16.png)

:::meta
Role | Product Designer
Team | Solo
Duration | 48 Hours
Tools | Figma, Notion, Figjam
Coffee | 4 Cups
:::

Thanks Sriram for considering my application, Happy reading!

## Quick Review

[Read - 5 mins] I know time is precious, So I gave a entire summary here end to end about the process which I gone through and it is much easy to read and if you want to go more in depth of how I approached you are welcome to scroll down and start reading.

### Profile tab

The redesign process involved crafting each page element: starting with the profile, where space was optimized for settings and a cohesive color scheme was implemented. Next, the progress bar was simplified for clarity, featuring a distinct progress number. The word of the day card was revamped to include pronunciation aids and a clickable icon. Finally, the gifts card was streamlined with a Copy CTA, enhancing accessibility for users to share codes effortlessly. This iterative process aimed to create a more intuitive, user-friendly experience while maintaining a clean and minimalistic design throughout the app.

### Word of the day

The process began with enhancing the app pronunciation feature, adding alternative meanings and examples for better understanding. Next, I revamped the user interface by changing the text to Start Now and integrating a Save button for word recall. Then, I proposed increasing the questionnaire difficulty to extend user sessions. Finally, I designed an interactive empty box, aiming for a cleaner UI and potential drag-and-drop functionality, enhancing user experience and engagement with the app features.

## 01 Start

I knew Stimuler before, as I am an undergrad, and I had an idea of doing a master in design. Then I came to know there is a thing called IELTS. I need to clear this and the GRE part as well. When I enquired with my consultant about the exams, the quotation was out of the syllabus. I did not have the budget to join and learn, although after getting a quote for my entire study, I got my plans back, and I planned to work for a while after my graduation. Meanwhile, I still see online resources that give coaching for the GRE and IELTS. Then I came to Magoosh, Duolingo, and Stimuler. The price of these apps was very minimal, but I always have an uncertainness towards them because I always see their worth and value for money, and I also personally do not like online mode of education.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_7.png)

I have not used Stimuler that time, but now that I have an assignment to work on, I have been using Stimuler for now, and to be honest, the UI is really nice and crisp. I have tried every page possible to find any design-related issue, and I found a few of them, you can see in my coming process.

### Goal

The goal is to redesign the profile tab and a feature called word of the day, both of which needed a revamp. I have ideas to implement, but I also need to look at the metrics and retention. It is also mentioned to increase the session length on the word of the day feature.

## 02 Initial Finding

I initially felt like Cred because the app itself has a lot of micro-interactions. It has a very black-and-white-focused app, and it is an edtech app, so it should be focused enough. Apart from that, I got a lot of issues as part of the user experience: contrast, accessibility, typography, and much more, but I will not cover that because the goal is to redesign the requirement.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/WhatsApp_Image_2024-05-03_at_12.00.55_PM_(1).jpeg)

Home Page

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/WhatsApp_Image_2024-05-03_at_12.00.55_PM_(2).jpeg)

Talk Page

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/WhatsApp_Image_2024-05-03_at_12.00.59_PM_(1).jpeg)

Profile Page

I liked those streaks and gamification on the app to push users to use more on the practice and maintaining the interest to reach mini goals.

The colors are very dark as if sometimes I miss things here and the interactions are quite confusing because if you use lists, buttons will be helpful to interact but in this case everything has a card design and it also has button, so I do not feel using buttons in a card.

### Competitors

I tried the alternatives of Stimuler.

- Duolingo [Top Rated]

- Speak ELSA

- Lingo Kids [Kids based]

## 03 My Process

I always use this process to enhance any app or feature and it is a generic flow and I usually follow to extract good metrics in the output.

### Type of Product

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_1.png)

- a. Mobile Application [Android and iOS] — This is a mobile application basically and it also offers users to download in play store and app store. It has over good reviews and ranked top apps from google play.

- b. Ed-Tech for Language [English] — As the application itself suggests that it is used for learning English in a easier way using AI and chat based flows.

- c. Interactive app [Tap, See, Hear, Speak] — It has new type of interactions like hear and speak.

### Core problem or Goal

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_2.png)

- a. Users Retention — Users are skipping again and again, can not retain at any feature. It has to be increased to maintain the session length.

- b. UI Fix — Contrast, Typography and Interaction Delay.

### Users

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_3.png)

Generally there only two types of users for this application, as it has a subscription model only those who interested to develop their speaking skills will be using and if some one who are influenced by past user will be using roughly.

- a. IELTS, TOFEL Aspirants

- b. General Audience [Includes above age of 15]

### Metrics

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_4.png)

- a. Direct Metric [Retention, Task Success rate] — It can be increased by giving more insights on particular thing and providing option for the users to select their preferences. It can be also increased by structuring and bringing things which are interactable and more prioritized. Guiding every task would make users to go through the process.

## 04 User Flow

For instance, I have designed a user flow for a specific persona to find errors and try to solve in context to profile tab and word of the day.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_12.png)

I used a fictional persona, so I can make a user flow according to her character and bringing some possible errors, I have gone through like someone who starts using app and something which they see and feel.

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_13.png)

Through the user flow, I got new three problems and I have written my suggestions to overcome these problem. By using these flows, I could make a better flow and fill the voids which is arising.

## 05 Ideation

There are many ways to showcase the visual elements, and Stimuler has shadow and 2 surface colors to differentiate and designed in a modern way. There is air in design and does not feel cluttered.

As the application is well designed and it just needs a revamp, So we will go by the Neilson and Norman way — Usually Choose Incremental Changes Over a Major Overhaul.

I always look for applications and websites and I have mentioned in my works as well, because the person who has done before would have decided for the requirement and as a product designer we need to respect it.

### Profile Tab

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_8.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/8e873025-9976-4bba-9ea8-a9cfdd3fb973.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_6.png)

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_9.png)

While Ideating profile tab I got ideas to shift components here and there to find the perfect spot and then I started drafting wireframes. In profile tab I got five components: Settings, Progress Bar, Word of the day, Practice saved words, Gift referral.

At first, the progress bar is not interactable and scrolling down I could see cards which is interactable and some mandate features of app. So I grabbed all the mandate cards first and decreased the size of PFP added settings near to it.

Secondly, I reduced space in between cards and brought things near, so the users can actually see something new everyday. The tab is named as profile but it has every good component for a aspiring learner.

My Suggestion would be making the progress bar to minimal and giving priority to the actual features of the app.

### Word of the day

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_11.png)

Word of the day is a very interesting concept but due to lack of good typeface weight it seems very much odd. I liked the whole process of learning new words by synonyms and antonyms, preferably the confetti and sounds after clicking options are very much interactive. I also had ideas to drag and drop but as the age group is above 15, It would be fancier.

The next step after tapping word of the day, the card changes to grey or disabled state and it gives some off to volume icon. After moving I got a chat image saying how to choose answers, and then those questions. I feel it very simple and it should be. As soon I answer at the first, it actually ended soon but It adds streak which is again a goal encouraging to make more use of application.

My suggestion would be to add two more question to increase session length and add quality sounds and based on people how feel encouraging hearing those sounds. If possible adding vibration to wrong ones can be more interactive.

## 06 Design

The main part of the assignment, I used Figma for designing this project. Before going to design, I drafted few wireframes which works out with relatable to reach any metric or enhancing users experience.

I also checked reviews on google play if someone who face difficulty in using app, but I found reviews that people faced issues in flow and onboarding, some faced issues in subscription due to technical glitch apart from that I did not find any issues from users complaining on interactions.

### Wireframes

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_10.png)

I drafted a low fidelity wireframe on the profile tab and checked some competitors app and I found that every concept is framed according to the mental model. The streak, word of the day, detailed analysis on sessions are given. As we follow the design principle rule, I started designing after completing the wireframe.

### Design Suggestions — Profile Tab

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_14.png)

As I have discussed earlier, I have designed the pages, I have also highlighted the component with numbers and I will elaborate in detail.

- The profile needed a revamp because, the old design was not good and it took lot of space, I took the PFP and added space for settings with icon. The surface color and the primary was matching quite same. It will surely elevate a minimal and clean look and encourage users and tells user that the settings are there.

- The progress bar is changed because it is not an interactable component and only it can show information to the user and it give a goal oriented feel to user. I have removed the speed-o-meter and to minimal bar line. I have changed your progress number with gradient stroke so it could be distinct from the CTA.

- I have changed the card style of word of the day and added quite a few details related to the word. As we saw a persona and as a beginner it is mandate to have the pronunciation clearly so this would surely help the user to learn better. Also there is a right icon to denote it is a clickable card.

- I found gifts for you card slightly not matching the app but to make it more minimal and accessible I have added a CTA called Copy for ease to copy code and refer friends.

### Design Suggestions — Word of the Day

![Image](/gallery/ExportBlock-ac999e04-d396-481e-af51-c4cf8f795c02-Part-1/Case studies/Stimuler - UX Enhancement/Image_15.png)

Previously we were discussing about the profile tab revamp, Here I have redesigned the word of the day card.

- As we saw there is addon of pronunciation tags, I have further added alternatives because If a user does not know the meaning but it is same as some other word and it could be easy to understand. Following this there is example in next screens, so alternatives can be best part.

- I have changed the text to Start Now instead of practice which is very lame, I have added another CTA before that and It is a save button for saving words into dictionary, so that the user can recall any day.

- To increase the session length I would suggest to increase questionnaire ranging from easy to difficult, so If the user is a beginner they can even get a few answers correct and try to achieve it daily.

- I have added an empty box which gives a slight clean design in terms of UI and the user can tap or even drag and drop the box over there.

## 07 Conclusion

I have made my suggestions which improvise the overall enhancement and usability by crafting for all types of users and maintaining retention metrics. These always help users to have a better experience than the older one. Coming to my experience with Stimuler is quite good, I have scanned whole app and I get new ideas to implement in each page but it all can be possible by testing out users and analyzing mental models.

### Improvement

There is always scope of improvement as It can be designed much better if focused on accessibility part, every UI is good until unless it is rolled to users and they feel comfortable using it. While I was using other competitor apps, I found designs to be same everywhere and the concept of using similar approach would be the users are trained to specific mental models for English learning apps, If it is then Stimuler can work on adding special avatar or a character which brings life and enhances the gamification much more.

Thankyou for reading till the end!

title: UX Enhancement - Spaarks
text:
I tested this app from end to end and recorded my observations sequentially as a user journey. I focused primarily on usability and accessibility, covering both interface design and user flow. Although the app feels like a blend of Twitter, Reddit, and Instagram, I kept my suggestions unbiased, focusing purely on UX best practices rather than personal habits.

### Initial Findings

Before starting, I researched the product online to understand its context. During testing, I noted that the app uses shades of blue as its primary color and gradients for its Call-to-Actions (CTAs). I identified several friction points: the Android back gesture does not reliably navigate back, the search bar has a small target height, users cannot select images after typing, and the CTAs lack clear active, hovered, or disabled states.

### Onboarding & Splash Screen

Upon opening the app, I encountered the splash screen and onboarding pages, but the swipe transitions were unresponsive when navigating left and right.

Expanding the onboarding from two pages to three or four would help introduce the app's features more thoroughly, building user confidence.

Currently, both pages use similar illustrations; they should be replaced with distinct visuals representing the specific features of the app.

### Home and Navbar

While the navbar icons are generally consistent, the marketplace icon behaves unpredictably when switching pages. Outline icons should consistently fill when active to reflect the current page state.

The "Add" icon changes to a shopping bag icon in the marketplace, which could be simplified to be more minimal.

The search bar is quite small; enlarging its touch target will prevent users from accidentally tapping adjacent story elements.

### Marketplace

Image swiping is sluggish and occasionally misinterprets horizontal gestures, accidentally redirecting the user to the jobs section. (Tested on a 60Hz display).

The product listing uses violet text for differentiation, which conflicts with the color system. The "Your Products" header could be enlarged instead of introducing non-brand colors.

The CTAs in the marketplace need polish for better visual hierarchy.

The "Save" button should be placed directly on the product card for quick access, rather than hiding it inside a kebab menu.

### Post and others

In the post creation flow, the delete icon in the basic image editor is inconsistent and too small to tap easily. After publishing, captions appear directly above the image with very little padding. A separator line would help distinguish the username from the caption.

Toast notifications and small dialog cards should stretch to full grid width and sit slightly above the bottom navbar.

The comments panel should support a swipe-down gesture to dismiss, rather than relying solely on a close button.

Additionally, displaying the active account name at the top of the post editor would help users managing multiple accounts.

### UI Fix

CTAs should use solid brand colors instead of gradients to improve aesthetic appeal.

Vignettes should be added to the story editor to improve text readability over varied background colors.

Icon choices should use familiar, recognizable symbols with rounded visual styles.

Settings menu items should have increased spacing and larger tap targets for a better user experience.

Dialog boxes and transition animations can be polished to elevate the overall visual quality.

Ensure all icons are visually consistent and use a unified rounded style.

Placeholder text opacity should be reduced to differentiate it from user input.

### Verdict

To gather deeper insights, I reviewed user feedback on the Play Store and App Store. While most reviews focused on technical issues rather than UX specifically, a heuristic evaluation based on Nielsen Norman guidelines highlights key areas for refinement. These recommendations are offered with respect for the original design and development team to help polish the app's user experience.

:::verdict
Typography can be improved with proper sizing and line spacing.
The content page can be simplified by reducing the opacity of the background image.
The "Vision and Values" section should be redesigned to improve legibility.
The rounded rectangles in the brand framework can be replaced with a cleaner layout.
Additional breathing room and whitespace should be added between paragraph blocks on each slide.
The color palette label contrast should be adjusted.
The corners of the Spaarks logo should be softened.
Overall alignment and spacing should be checked system-wide.
:::
